title: Anthropic disables Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after U.S. export-control directive
friendlyTitle: Two Anthropic models are being switched off after a U.S. order
summary: Anthropic said the U.S. government issued an export-control directive requiring suspension of access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals. Because the company says it cannot practically enforce that limit user by user, it is disabling the two models for all customers while leaving other Anthropic models unaffected.
friendlySummary: Anthropic says a U.S. government order requires it to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals. The company says the practical result is that it must disable the two models for everyone for now.
whyItMatters: This is a major signal that AI governance is moving from chips and infrastructure into model access itself. For businesses and developers, it reinforces the need for fallback models, portability, and procurement plans that can survive sudden access changes.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If your work depends on one frontier model, access can change quickly. Teams should have backup models and workflows ready instead of assuming every model will always be available.
